pattern: Track the current non-decreasing vowel run and how many distinct vowels it covers, resetting whenever the alphabetical order breaks.
hints: A valid window can only grow while each next character is equal to or the next vowel after the previous one.
Reset the run whenever the order is violated; otherwise extend it and count how many distinct vowels you have seen.
Keep a current length and a distinct-vowel count; a window is beautiful only once it has used all five vowels, so record its length then.

edge_inputs.description: single character can never be beautiful
exactly the five vowels once each
order breaks then a full beautiful run follows
repeated vowels extend the longest beautiful run
a near-miss run resets when order regresses
multiple beautiful runs, the longer one wins

stress.description: random vowel string, frequently producing valid and invalid runs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    vowels = 'aeiou'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(vowels) for _ in range(n))]
stress.note: pure random vowels; the optimal scan runs in linear time

stress.description: many concatenated beautiful and broken runs
stress.note: constructed runs so beautiful windows actually appear
